title: Month 1 major mock: the market
canDo: Introduce myself: name, greeting, what I want (Week 1).
Talk about daily actions and objects using the accusative (Week 2).
Say what I have and don't have using genitive (Week 3).
Talk about my family using мой/моя (Week 3).
Count, ask prices, and tell my age (Week 4).

exercises.prompt: Match the Russian to its meaning — one from each week.
Fill in the correct case-marked form.

items.q: Hello! My name is ... Nice to meet you.
I always buy bread and milk.
items.answer: Я всегда покупаю хлеб и молоко.

items.q: I have a big family, but little money.
items.answer: У меня большая семья, но мало денег.

items.q: How much does this cost? — Fifty rubles.
How old are you? — I'm thirty.
I never work on the weekend.
items.answer: Я никогда не работаю в выходные.

exercises.prompt: Put the words in the right order.

speakingTask.prompt: Roleplay a market visit, out loud, as if to a real shopkeeper. Cover all four: (1) greet them and ask what they have — «У вас есть...?», (2) ask the price of at least two items — «Сколько стоит...?», (3) react to a price and ask for a quantity — «Дайте, пожалуйста, два...» or similar, (4) somewhere in the conversation, mention your age naturally, the way it might come up in small talk.
rubric: Greeted naturally and asked if the shop has something specific
Asked at least two prices using сколько стоит
Reacted to a price (дорого/дёшево) at least once
Used a number + noun correctly (watch the 1/2-4/5+ endings)
Whole exchange held together as one continuous conversation, not disconnected sentences
